examples: Venus flytraps, for example, share an ancestor with Portuguese sundews, which only catch prey passively, via "flypaper" glands on their stems.
Similarly, a digital clock is powered with the same type of microbial fuel cell, collecting its prey with flypaper.
But earwax seems to have the very humble role of being no more than biological flypaper, serving to prevent dust and insects entering the ear.
There are novels that are contraptions, configured like cages, traps, or flypaper, to catch things and hold them.
Excrement from millions of chickens found its way into waterways and attracted so many flies that nearby homeowners hung flypaper in their rooms.
